description: Opportunity matrix for FrankenLibC optimization waves. Scores hotspots on three dimensions (impact, confidence, effort) to produce a composite score. Each candidate now carries replayable golden-output verification, rollback instructions, and attribution metadata so every admitted lever stays one-change-at-a-time, proof-backed, and reversible.

impact.description: Expected improvement magnitude. Combines call frequency, current overhead, and user-visible effect.

anchors.1.0: Minor: <5% reduction in overhead for coldpath APIs
anchors.2.0: Moderate: 5-20% reduction for hotpath, or eliminates a call-through
anchors.3.0: Significant: >20% reduction for hotpath, or eliminates multiple call-throughs
anchors.4.0: Major: enables new replacement level (L1→L2 transition)
anchors.5.0: Critical: removes last blocker for replacement level transition

confidence.description: Certainty that the optimization will achieve claimed impact. Based on profiling data, benchmarks, or prior art.

anchors.2.0: Comparable system shows improvement (e.g., musl, bionic)
anchors.3.0: Prototype benchmark shows improvement on synthetic workload
anchors.4.0: Criterion benchmark shows improvement on representative workload
anchors.5.0: Production trace confirms improvement with statistical significance

effort.description: Implementation complexity and risk. Higher score = less effort (inverted so high total score = desirable).

anchors.0.0: Requires new subsystem (rtld, CRT, NSS backend)
anchors.5.0: Trivial: flag change, constant tuning, config update

scoring.threshold_policy: Items with score >= 2.0 are eligible for implementation. Items below 2.0 are deferred unless a justification_waiver is provided.

entries.title: Eliminate locale_abi stubs (setlocale, localeconv)
entries.rationale: Eliminates 2 stubs blocking L1 transition. C/POSIX locale is well-defined, implementation is bounded.

invariants: Locale support classification remains synchronized with support_matrix.json
Locale fixture coverage remains replayable before and after the lever lands

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only locale_abi stub-elimination changes plus regenerated opportunity/proof artifacts.

entries.title: Eliminate resolv_abi stubs (getaddrinfo, getnameinfo, freeaddrinfo, gai_strerror)
entries.rationale: Eliminates 4 stubs blocking L1. Numeric-only backend is feasible but DNS requires significant work.

invariants: Resolver fixture results stay aligned with the declared support state
Strict and hardened resolver behaviors stay replayable through the conformance matrix

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the resolv_abi stub-elimination lever and regenerated wave artifacts.

entries.title: Migrate dlfcn_abi to raw syscall (dlopen, dlclose, dlsym, dlerror)
entries.rationale: Required for L2 transition. ELF relocation + symbol lookup is complex but well-documented.

invariants: dlfcn boundary policy remains synchronized with support matrix state
Dynamic-loading edge cases remain covered by the dlfcn policy artifact

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the dlfcn raw-syscall migration lever and regenerated proof artifacts.

entries.title: Futex-based pthread_mutex_* (lock, unlock, trylock, init, destroy)
entries.rationale: Eliminates 5+ call-throughs from hotpath. Futex mutex is well-understood (musl, bionic precedent).

invariants: Mutex strict and hardened fixture artifacts remain replayable
Per-wave mutex behavior remains inside the existing perf and semantics guards

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the mutex hot-path lever plus regenerated opportunity/proof artifacts.

entries.title: Thread bootstrap (pthread_create, pthread_join, pthread_detach)
entries.rationale: Eliminates 3 critical call-throughs. TLS handoff is the hard part.

invariants: Thread lifecycle stress evidence remains replayable in strict and hardened modes
Thread bootstrap lever retains the current lifecycle semantics and teardown guarantees

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the thread bootstrap lever plus regenerated opportunity/proof artifacts.

entries.title: SIMD-accelerated memcpy/memmove/memset
entries.rationale: Current Rust std implementations are already good. Marginal gain from explicit AVX2/AVX-512.

invariants: String-memory fixture outputs remain byte-for-byte stable
SIMD wave must preserve memcpy/memmove semantic boundaries before any merge

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the SIMD string lever plus regenerated opportunity/proof artifacts.

entries.title: CRT bootstrap (_start, __libc_start_main)
entries.rationale: Critical for L3 (full replacement) but very high effort. Assembly-level bootstrap required.

invariants: Startup fixture coverage remains replayable before the CRT lever changes
Bootstrap semantics stay attributable to a single subsystem-implementation wave

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the CRT/bootstrap lever and regenerated wave artifacts.

entries.title: Env variable functions (getenv, setenv, unsetenv)
entries.rationale: Simple to implement but low call frequency. Nice L1 hygiene improvement.

invariants: Environment-function support claims remain synchronized with the conformance matrix
Wave attribution stays limited to stdlib environment semantics

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only getenv/setenv/unsetenv lever changes and regenerated wave artifacts.

entries.title: NSS files backend for passwd/group/hosts
entries.rationale: Required for L3 but complex. File parsing + caching + thread safety.

invariants: passwd/group/NSS fixture outputs remain replayable
NSS files lever remains attributable to one subsystem-implementation wave

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the NSS files backend lever and regenerated proof/opportunity artifacts.

entries.title: Resolver UDP backend
entries.rationale: Required for L3. UDP DNS with timeout/retry is well-specified but needs careful implementation.

invariants: Resolver fixture parity remains replayable across libc comparisons
UDP backend changes stay isolated to a single subsystem-implementation wave

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the resolver UDP backend lever and regenerated wave artifacts.

entries.title: Lookup table ctype (isalpha, isdigit, etc.)
entries.rationale: Already Implemented. 256-byte lookup table could improve branch prediction for ASCII classification.

invariants: ctype fixture answers remain stable across cache-layout experiments
ASCII classification semantics remain unchanged while cache behavior improves

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the ctype cache-optimization lever and regenerated wave artifacts.

entries.title: Native stdio (fopen, fread, fwrite, etc.)
entries.rationale: Required for L3. FILE* reimplementation is massive (30 symbols). Very high effort offsets high impact.

invariants: stdio fixture outputs remain replayable for the declared FILE* surface
Native stdio stays attributable to one subsystem-implementation wave

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the stdio subsystem-implementation lever and regenerated wave artifacts.

entries.title: T7.1 deterministic strict+hardened hotspot seeding for one-lever optimization waves
entries.rationale: Uses deterministic profile artifacts (target/profiles/20260213T083559Z strict+hardened hotspot_opportunity_matrix.v1.json) to seed high-score opportunities and unblock T7.2/T8.1 execution with repeatable evidence.

invariants: Hotspot seeding remains grounded in deterministic baseline artifacts
Wave admission stays tied to replayable strict and hardened latency evidence

rollback_instructions.expected_revert_scope: Only the hotspot-seeding lever and regenerated opportunity/proof artifacts.

highest_score.title: T7.1 deterministic strict+hardened hotspot seeding for one-lever optimization waves
